subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: A continuous random variable has the pdf $f(x) = \begin{cases} \frac{600}{x^2}, & 100 < x < 120 \\ 0, & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}$ (a) Determine the mean and variance of $X$. (c) Find $\Pr(X > 110)$.
explanation: (a) We have $E(X) = \int_{100}^{120} x \cdot 600 x^{-2}\,dx = 600 \ln x\big|_{100}^{120} \approx 109.39$ and $\sigma^2 = \int_{100}^{120} x^2 \cdot 600 x^{-2}\,dx - 109.39^2 \approx 33.19$. (b) The desired probability is $\Pr(X > 110) = \int_{110}^{120} 600 x^{-2}\,dx = \frac{5}{11}$. Sometimes for technical purposes the following theorem is useful. It expresses the expectation in terms of an integral of probabilities. It is most often used for random variables $X$ that have only positive values; in that case the second term is of course zero.
